2606.11961.title: Categorical Prior Lock-in: Why In-Context Learning Fails for Structured Data
2606.11961.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as conditional generators for structured data, relying on in-context learning (ICL) to adapt to new distributions without parameter updates. We investigate the limits of ICL for structured generation under distribution mismatch, using high-cardinality tabular data as a controlled test case, and identify a structural failure mode we term \textit{categorical prior lock-in}: the inability of ICL to update the model's prior over token distributions inherited from pre-training. Across two 7B-parameter open-weight models, ICL improves numerical fidelity with additional examples but exhibits a sharp ceiling on categorical distributions, failing to reproduce rare classes entirely. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (LoRA) overcomes these limitations but introduces measurable memorization risk and, in some cases, destabilizes structured output generation, highlighting a fundamental trade-off between adaptability and privacy.
2606.11961.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作結構化數據的條件生成器，依賴於上下文學習（ICL）來適應新分佈而無需參數更新。<lb/>我們研究了在分佈不匹配的情況下，ICL對結構生成的限制，使用高基數的表格數據作為受控測試案例，並確定了一種我們稱之為\textit{類別先驗鎖定}的結構性失效模式：ICL無法更新從預訓練中繼承的標記分佈的模型先驗。<lb/>在兩個7B參數的開放權重模型中，ICL通過額外示例提高了數值真實性，但在類別分佈上顯示出明顯的上限，完全無法重現稀有類別。<lb/>參數高效的微調（LoRA）克服了這些限制，但引入了可測量的記憶風險，在某些情況下，還會使結構化輸出生成不穩定，突顯了適應性與隱私之間的基本權衡。
2606.11961.author: Antonio Pelusi et.al.
2606.11961.authors: Antonio Pelusi, Stefano Braghin, Alberto Trombetta
